2603.30033.title: Tucker Attention: A generalization of approximate attention mechanisms
2603.30033.paper_summary: The pursuit of reducing the memory footprint of the self-attention mechanism in multi-headed self attention (MHA) spawned a rich portfolio of methods, e.g., group-query attention (GQA) and multi-head latent attention (MLA). The methods leverage specialized low-rank factorizations across embedding dimensions or attention heads. From the point of view of classical low-rank approximation, these methods are unconventional and raise questions of which objects they really approximate and how to interpret the low-rank behavior of the resulting representations. To answer these questions, this work proposes a generalized view on the weight objects in the self-attention layer and a factorization strategy, which allows us to construct a parameter efficient scheme, called Tucker Attention. Tucker Attention requires an order of magnitude fewer parameters for comparable validation metrics, compared to GQA and MLA, as evaluated in LLM and ViT test cases. Additionally, Tucker Attention~encompasses GQA, MLA, MHA as special cases and is fully compatible with flash-attention and rotary position embeddings (RoPE). This generalization strategy yields insights of the actual ranks achieved by MHA, GQA, and MLA, and further enables simplifications for MLA.
2603.30033.paper_summary_zh: 自注意機制在多頭自注意（MHA）中減少記憶體佔用的追求催生了一系列豐富的方法，例如群查詢注意（GQA）和多頭潛在注意（MLA）。這些方法利用嵌入維度或注意頭的專門低秩分解。從經典低秩近似的角度來看，這些方法是不尋常的，並引發了它們實際上近似哪些對象以及如何解釋所得到的表示的低秩行為的問題。為了回答這些問題，本研究提出了一種對自注意層中權重對象的概括性觀點和一種分解策略，這使我們能夠構建一種稱為塔克注意的參數高效方案。與GQA和MLA相比，塔克注意在可比較的驗證指標下需要的參數數量少一個量級，這在LLM和ViT測試案例中得到了評估。此外，塔克注意還包括GQA、MLA和MHA作為特例，並且與閃存注意和旋轉位置嵌入（RoPE）完全兼容。這種概括策略提供了對MHA、GQA和MLA實際達到的秩的洞察，並進一步使MLA的簡化成為可能。
2603.30033.author: Timon Klein et.al.
2603.30033.authors: Timon Klein, Jonas Kusch, Sebastian Sager, Stefan Schnake, Steffen Schotthöfer
